title: libpod/networking_linux.go: switch to sha256 hash generation
body:
SHA-1 is prone to collisions.

This will likely break connectivity between old containers started
before update and containers started after update. It will also fail to
cleanup old netns. A reboot will fix this, so a reboot is recommended
after update.

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]


Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Is a reboot required or just restarting old containers?
based on the potential breakage, is this a 5.0 delivery?

We should get rid of sha1 sooner than later, the risk to break someone is very low. And restarting the contianer should fix it, maybe even `podman network reload --all`. Until you reboot you will have a one leaked netns around but this will not cause any issues.

We can not wait for 5.0, and I do not see this as a huge problem.

/lgtm
are we ok to backport this patch to v4.0-rhel ?
Probably? We do have a BZ to justify.

Is 4.0 already released on RHEL? If not then it would be a good to backport because 4.0 already breaks this with the change from one rootless netns to one netns per libpod db.
However if it is already used we should not break this via backports. Doing it with 4.1 sounds more reasonable to me.

I don't think you can stop the RHEL ship, and I don't see this as a big enough deal to put the brakes on.  Fix it in podman 4.1 which ships in 12 weeks.  It is likely that you will find other sha1 issues.
